Details: The first available version of the API

Resources.Description: Individual event attendance for a person.

Attributes.Description: Whether or not the person attended the event.

The role of the person at the time of event.


Possible values: `member`, `leader`, `visitor`, or `applicant`

CanInclude.Description: Include related `Person` resources in the response.

CanOrderBy.Description: Sort response items by the `FirstName` attribute.
Sort response items by the `LastName` attribute.
Sort response items by the `Role` attribute.

CanQueryBy.Description: Query response items by the `Role` attribute.

Resources.Description: A campus as defined in Planning Center Accounts

Attributes.Description: The name of the campus

CanOrderBy.Description: Sort response items by the `Name` attribute.

CanQueryBy.Description: Query response items by the `Id` attribute.
Query response items by the `Name` attribute.

Resources.Description: Details on how and when members can join a `Group`.

Attributes.Description: Whether or not enrollment has been closed automatically due to set limits
Brief description as to which limit automatically closed enrollment
Date when enrollment should automatically close
Whether or not the `date_limit` has been reached
Total number of members allowed before enrollment should automatically close
Whether or not the `member_limit` has been reached

Current enrollment status. Possible values:
* `open` - strategy is not `closed` and no limits have been reached
* `closed` - strategy is `closed` _or_ limits have been reached
* `full` - member limit has been reached
* `private` - group is unlisted
Sign up strategy. Possible values: `request_to_join`, `open_signup`, or `closed`

Resources.Description:
An event is a meeting of a group. It has a start and end time, and can be
either physical or virtual.

Attributes.Description:
This is a group setting that applies to all the events in the group.
If selected, an email will be sent to the primary email address of the group leader
60 minutes before the event start time, asking them to report attendance.

If `true`, we send an event remind some specified time before the event starts
to all members in the group.
Whether or not the event has been canceled.
The date and time the event was canceled.
A longform description of the event. Can contain HTML markup.
The date and time the event ends.
The image for the event.
Either "physical" or "virtual".
`true` if the event spans multiple days. Otherwise `false`.
The name/title of the event.
`true` if reminders have been sent for this event. Otherwise `false`.
The date and time reminders were sent for this event.
`true` if the event is a repeating event. Otherwise `false`.
The date and time the event starts.

The URL for the virtual location. Typically we don't show this URL unless
unless the location_type_preference is "virtual".

The number of visitors who attended the event. These are people who are not
members of the group.

Filters.Description: where `attended` is `true`

CanInclude.Description: Include related `Group` resources in the response.
Include related `Location` resources in the response.

CanOrderBy.Description: Sort response items by the `EndsAt` attribute.
Sort response items by the `Name` attribute.
Sort response items by the `StartsAt` attribute.

CanQueryBy.Description: Query response items by the `EndsAt` attribute.
Query response items by the `Name` attribute.
Query response items by the `StartsAt` attribute.

Resources.Description: Notes that group leaders can write for an event, generally related to attendance.

Attributes.Description: The body text of the note.

CanInclude.Description: Include related `Owner` resources in the response.

Resources.Description: A group of people that meet together regularly.

Attributes.Description: The date and time the group was archived.

A boolean representing the current user's authorization to start a new conversation in the group.


Only available when requested with the `?fields` param

A code representing why the current user can or cannot start a new conversation in the group.
Possible values: `chat_disabled`, `group_archived`, `non_member`, `leaders_only`.
Nil when the user is permitted to create a conversation.


Only available when requested with the `?fields` param
A boolean representing whether or not the group has Chat enabled.

If a contact_email is provided, we will display a contact button on the public page
where potential members can ask questions before joining the group.
The date and time the group was created.
A longform description of the group. Can contain HTML markup.
The plain text version of the group description.

Calculated value. Either `true` or `false`.
It will be `false` if any of these conditions are true:
* The group is archived
* The group is full (has reached specified enrollment limit)
* Enrollment Strategy is `closed`
* Enrollment deadline has passed

The enrollment strategy for the group.
See [documentation](https://pcogroups.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360016079154#Visibility&EnrollmentStatus)
for more information about group enrollment settings.


Possible values: `closed`, `request_to_join`, or `open_signup`
Whether or not events are visible to the public on Church Center

The visibility of events for the group.


Possible values: `public` or `members`

A hash of header image URLs. The keys are `thumbnail`, `medium`, and `original`.

```json
{
  "thumbnail": "https://groups-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/group/header_image/1986065/thumbnail_image-1676676396838.jpg",
  "medium": "https://groups-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/group/header_image/1986065/medium_image-1676676396838.jpg",
  "original": "https://groups-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/group/header_image/1986065/image-1676676396838.jpg"
}
```

Whether or not group leaders have access to the entire
church database on the admin side of Groups. (Not recommended)
Whether or not the group is visible on Church Center

The location type preference for the group.


Possible values: `physical` or `virtual`
Whether or not group members can see other members' info

The number of members in the group, includes leaders.
Does not include membership requests.
The name/title of the group.
The public URL for the group on Church Center.

A text summary of the group's typical meeting schedule.
Can be a string like "Sundays at 9:30am" or "Every other Tuesday at 7pm".

The IDs of the tags associated with the group.


Only available when requested with the `?fields` param

The URL for the group's virtual location. A zoom link, for example.
This could be set even if `location_type_preference` is `physical`.
This is useful if you want to display a zoom link even if the group is meeting in person.

DEPRECATED: This is a private attribute used by Home widgets that we plan to remove soon.


Only available when requested with the `?fields` param

Actions.Requirements: Must be an administrator, group type manager, or leader.
Actions.Description: Planning Center does not provide a description for this action.
Actions.AdditionalDetails:
This action can be used to assign campuses to a group.
All campuses will be replaced so the full data set must be sent.

Actions.Requirements:
Can be run by administrators or group type managers,
as well as leaders if allowed by the group type's settings.
Actions.Description: Planning Center does not provide a description for this action.
Actions.AdditionalDetails:
Disables chat for the given group. This will hide chat from the group.
You must confirm your intention with a `confirm` attribute to allow this action to succeed.

Actions.Requirements: Can be run by administrators or group type managers.
Actions.Description: Planning Center does not provide a description for this action.
Actions.AdditionalDetails:
Starts a background job to duplicate a group along with selected settings.

Accepts an optional `name` for the new group. If not provided, the name will
default to the original group's name with " (Copy)" appended (e.g., "My Group (Copy)").
The new group name must be unique.

Requires a `fields` array specifying which aspects of the group to duplicate,
such as members, leaders, and settings.

Optionally accepts a `group_type_id` to assign a different group type to the
new group. If omitted, the original group's type will be used.

Actions.Requirements:
Can be run by administrators or group type managers,
as well as leaders if allowed by the group type's settings.
Actions.Description: Planning Center does not provide a description for this action.
Actions.AdditionalDetails:
Enables chat for the given group. Enabling chat will make the names of each member visible to
other group members. Chat cannot be enabled for a group if the member list is confidential.
You must confirm your intention with a `confirm` attribute to allow this action to succeed.

Filters.Description: have a `canceled_at` date and time
do not have a `canceled_at` date and time
only visible to group leaders

CanInclude.Description: Include related `Enrollment` resources in the response.
Include related `GroupType` resources in the response.
Include related `Location` resources in the response.

CanOrderBy.Description: Sort response items by the `ChatEnabled` attribute.
Sort response items by the `ContactEmail` attribute.
Sort response items by the `CreatedAt` attribute.
Sort response items by the `EnrollmentLimit` attribute.
Sort response items by the `Listed` attribute.
Sort response items by the `MembersAreConfidential` attribute.
Sort response items by the `MembershipsCount` attribute.
Sort response items by the `Name` attribute.

CanQueryBy.Description: Query response items by the `ArchiveStatus` attribute.
Query response items by the `Name` attribute.

Resources.Description: A group application is a request to join a group which can be approved or rejected.

Attributes.Description: Timestamp when this person applied.
An optional personal message from the applicant.

The approval status of the application.

Possible values: `pending`, `approved`, or `rejected`

Actions.Requirements: Can only be run on a pending application by an administrator, manager, or group leader
Actions.Description: Planning Center does not provide a description for this action.
Actions.AdditionalDetails:
This action can be used to approve a group application and immediately
add the person to the group. You can optionally provide a `role` attribute
as `member` (default) or `leader`. Only administrators or managers can
create leaders.

Actions.Requirements: Can only be run on a pending application by an administrator, manager, or group leader
Actions.Description: Planning Center does not provide a description for this action.
Actions.AdditionalDetails:
This action can be used to reject a group application. The person will not
be added to the group.

CanInclude.Description: Include related `Group` resources in the response.
Include related `Person` resources in the response.

CanOrderBy.Description: Sort response items by the `AppliedAt` attribute.
Sort response items by the `Status` attribute.

CanQueryBy.Description: Query response items by the `AppliedAt` attribute.
Query response items by the `Status` attribute.

Resources.Description:
A group type is a category of groups.
For example, a church might have group types for "Small Groups" and "Classes".

Attributes.Description: `true` if the map view is visible on the public groups list page. Otherwise `false`.
`true` if the group type contains any published groups. Otherwise `false`.

Hex color value. Color themes are a visual tool for administrators on the admin side of Groups.
Ex: "#4fd2e3"
A JSON object of default settings for groups of this type.
A description of the group type
The name of the group type
The position of the group type in relation to other group types.
The public URL for the group on Church Center.

Filters.Description: has a `canceled_at` date and time
do not have a `canceled_at` date and time
future `starts_at` date and time

CanOrderBy.Description: Sort response items by the `Name` attribute.
Sort response items by the `Position` attribute.

CanQueryBy.Description: Query response items by the `Id` attribute.

Attributes.Description:
This preference controls how the location is displayed to non-members for public groups and events.


Possible values: `hidden`, `approximate`, or `exact`

Ex: "1313 Disneyland Dr
Anaheim, CA 92802" (may be approximate or `null`)
Approximate address would be "Anaheim, CA 92802".
We obscure Canadian zip codes.
Ex: `33.815396` (may be approximate or `null`)
Ex: `-117.926399` (may be approximate or `null`)
Ex: "Disneyland"

The number of miles in a location's approximate address.
Will be `0` if the strategy is exact, and will be `null` if the strategy is hidden.

The display preference strategy used for the current request, based on user permissions.
Either `hidden`, `approximate`, or `exact`.

CanInclude.Description: Include related `Group` resources in the response.

Resources.Description: A person who belongs to a group.

Attributes.Description: The ID of the person in People.
The URL of the person's avatar.
The color theme of the group's group type.
The person's primary email address.
The person's first name.
The date and time the person joined the group.
The person's last name.
The person's primary phone number.

The role of the person in the group.
Possible values: `member` or `leader`

CanInclude.Description: Include related `Person` resources in the response.

CanOrderBy.Description: Sort response items by the `FirstName` attribute.
Sort response items by the `JoinedAt` attribute.
Sort response items by the `LastName` attribute.
Sort response items by the `Role` attribute.

CanQueryBy.Description: Query response items by the `Role` attribute.

Resources.Description: The organization represents a single church. Every other resource is scoped to this record.

Attributes.Description: The name of the organization.
The time zone of the organization.

Filters.Description:
from specific campuses; provide an additional `campus_id` param
as a comma-separated list of IDs, ex: `?filter=campus&campus_id=1,2,3`
has a `canceled_at` date and time

from specific groups; provide an additional `group_id` param
as a comma-separated list of IDs, ex: `?filter=group&group_id=1,2,3`

from specific group types; provide an additional `group_type_id` param
as a comma-separated list of IDs, ex: `?filter=group_type&group_type_id=1,2,3`
only group events of which you are a member
does not have a `canceled_at` date and time
future `starts_at` date and time
contains groups which are published to Church Center
does not contain any groups published to Church Center

from specific campuses; provide an additional `campus_id` param
as a comma-separated list of IDs, ex: `?filter=campus&campus_id=1,2,3`

filter groups with enrollment settings; provide an additional `enrollment` param
as a comma-separated list of values, ex: `?filter=enrollment&enrollment=open_signup,request_to_join,closed`

from specific groups; provide an additional `group_id` param
as a comma-separated list of IDs, ex: `?filter=group&group_id=1,2,3`

from specific group types; provide an additional `group_type_id` param
as a comma-separated list of IDs, ex: `?filter=group_type&group_type_id=1,2,3`
filter groups that are not published on Church Center
filter groups that have a meeting schedule
only groups of which you are a member

based on their setting of allowing leaders to search the entire
church database in Groups; provide an additional
`people_database_searchable` param with `only` or `none`,
ex: `?filter=people_database_searchable&people_database_searchable=only`
filter groups that are published on Church Center

filter groups with tags; provide an additional `tag_id` param
as a comma-separated list of IDs, ex: `?filter=tag&tag_id=1,2,3`.
filtering by tags within a tag group will expand results,
and across tag groups will reduce results.
where `display_publicly` is enabled

Resources.Description: The owner/creator of an event note.

Attributes.Description: The URL of the person's avatar.
The person's first name.
The person's last name.

Resources.Description:
A person is a user of Planning Center.
They can be a member of a group, a leader of a group, or an administrator.

Attributes.Description: Returns all the addresses associated with this person.
The URL of the person's avatar.

Whether or not the person is under 13 years old.
This is false if a birthdate is not set.


Only available when requested with the `?fields` param
Date and time this person was first created in Planning Center

Returns all the email addresses associated with this person.

```json
[{
  "address": "sam@example.com",
  "location": "Home",
  "primary": true
}]
```
The person's first name.
The person's gender (e.g. `Male`, `Female`).
The person's last name.
Can be `administrator`, `group_type_manager`, `leader`, `member`, or `no access`.

Returns all the phone numbers associated with this person.

```json
[{
  "number": "(800) 123-4567",
  "carrier": null,
  "location": "Mobile",
  "primary": true
}]
```

does not have a `canceled_at` date and time

Resources.Description:
A person's RSVP response for a group event.
RSVPs can be submitted at any point leading up to the event.
When an event reminder is sent, `awaiting_response` RSVPs are generated for
any remaining members, or `not_sent` if they're missing an email address.

Attributes.Description: Possible values: `awaiting_response`, `yes`, `no`, `maybe`, or `not_sent`

CanOrderBy.Description: Sort response items by the `Response` attribute.

CanQueryBy.Description: Query response items by the `Response` attribute.

Either `FileResource` or `LinkResource`
Possible values: `leaders` or `members`
